REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS BAYMARK FINANCIAL, INC., MARK HOWARD ENGELMAN AND VERA NOVOSEL'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT OR, ALTERNATIVELY, TO STRIKE THE PROTECTED PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT (SLAPP). ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO CARRY THEIR BURDEN AND SHOW THAT THE ENTIRE COMPLAINT OR ANY OF THE CLAIMS UPON WHICH RELIEF MAY BE GRANTED IS ASSERTED AGAINST THEM IN THE COMPLAINT ARISE OUT OF PROTECTED ACTIVITY (SERVICE OF NOTICE). THE COURT NOTES THAT ANALYSIS OF EACH CAUSE OF ACTION IS LIMITED TO 2-5 LINES, FOR EXAMPLE THE CLAIMS SEEKING CANCELLATION OF THE DEED OF TRUST, NOTICE OF DEFAULT AND NOTICE OF TRUSTEE'S SALE IS ASSERTED TO ARISE OUT OF PROTECTED ACTIVITY (SERVICE OF 3 DAY NOTICE) AS FOLLOWS: "THE CAUSES OF CAUSE OF ACTION FOR "CANCELLATION OF INSTRUMENTS" IS BASED, AT LEAST IN PART, ON PETITIONING ACTIVITY. IT INCORPORATES BY REFERENCE ALL 69 PARAGRAPHS THAT PRECEDE IT." PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-20-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Jak Marquez; Dowling & Marquez LLP; 415-977-0444; appearing as counsel for defendants Baymark Financial, Inc., Mark Howard Engelman, and Vera Novosel.
Sally L Liu; 415-635-1783; plaintiff, appearing in pro per.

Plaintiff contests the court's tentative ruling. Plaintiff requests that if tentative ruling is adopted, that she be granted 30 days leave to file her amended complaint. Defendant agrees to extending the time for plaintiff's leave to amend. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend to address in good faith all issues raised by the demurrer. The late filed opposition is stricken. Plaintiff shall have 30 days leave from Notice of Entry of Order to file amended complaint.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-26-2023 Continued To Oct-18-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Imposing Sanctions And For An Award Of Punitive Damages
Off Calendar Jun-12-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
No Appearance by Plaintiff Sally Liu.
Jak Marquez, Esq. 415-977-0444 Dowling & Marquez for Defendant.
No other Appearance.

RUING - The matter is discussed. The Court having reviewed the pleadings adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF SALLY LIU's Motion For Order Imposing Sanctions And For An Award Of Punitive Damages is DENIED. The Court does not find that the Anti-Slapp was frivolous.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (501/cfh)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-01-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS BAYMARK FINANCIAL, INC., MARK HOWARD ENGELMAN AND VERA NOVOSEL'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT OR, ALTERNATIVELY, TO STRIKE THE PROTECTED PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT (SLAPP)

Appearances: Jak Marquez of Dowling & Marquez, LLP (415.977.0444 x232) appearing via Zoom for defendants and moving parties; Sally Liu (415.635.1783) plaintiff and responding party appearing in propria persona.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DENIED. Moving party failed to carry their burden and show that the entire complaint or any of the claims upon which relief may be granted is asserted against them in the complaint arise out of protected activity (service of notice). The Court notes that analysis of each cause of action is limited to 2-5 lines, for example the claims seeking cancellation of the Deed of Trust, Notice of Default and Notice of Trustee's Sale is asserted to arise out of protected activity (service of 3 Day Notice) as follows: "The causes of cause of action for "cancellation of instruments" is based, at least in part, on petitioning activity. It incorporates by reference all 69 paragraphs that precede it." Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

Notice Of Defendants Baymark Financial Inc Mark Howard Engelman And Vera Novosels Special Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Complaint Or Alternatively To Strike The Protected Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint Slapp
